name: West Flanders (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic gold of the historic Flemish coveted shield
the heraldic azure of the historic Flemish coveted shield
the heraldic gules of the heart shield, after the arms reputedly borne by the House of Flanders before the Flemish Lion

description: A gyronny of twelve alternating yellow and blue triangles meeting at the centre of the flag, surcharged with a red oval escutcheon (heart shield) at the centre.
history: Approved on 27 May 1997 by decree of the Flemish Minister of Culture. The design revives the so-called coveted shield (gyronny of twelve, gold and azure, with a heart shield of gules) attributed by medieval armorials to the House of Flanders before the adoption of the Flemish Lion. The pattern is possibly derived from a misinterpreted ornamental fitting on the shield of Count Willem Clito (d. 1128) as depicted on his tomb in the Saint-Bertinus Abbey at Saint-Omer.

features.symbolism: gyronny of twelve, alternating yellow and blue triangles converging at the centre; this twelve-piece coveted pattern was attributed in medieval armorials to the House of Flanders

features.role: red heart shield (escutcheon en abyme) over the gyronny
features.symbolism: the gules escutcheon of the pre-Flemish-Lion Flemish arms
